# clawker

> Secure, isolated Docker environments for AI coding agents. A harness-agnostic, open-source AI container sandbox: run Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, OpenCode, Pi, or any other coding agent behind a deny-by-default kernel-side egress firewall (Envoy + CoreDNS + eBPF), with credential forwarding without exposing keys, git worktrees for parallel agents, and OpenTelemetry monitoring with cost dashboards. Extensible via bundles — Dockerfile template fragments plus config files that package harness setups, dev stacks, and monitoring extensions for hand-rolling or distribution (Copilot, Grok, z.ai, and more can be bundled in). Works with any model your harness runs. MIT licensed, written in Go, macOS and Linux.

This site (clawker.dev) is the project splash page. Full documentation lives at docs.clawker.dev, which also serves an llms.txt and a markdown twin of every page (append .md).

## Documentation

- [Quickstart](https://docs.clawker.dev/quickstart): install, init a project, run your first containerized agent
- [Threat Model](https://docs.clawker.dev/threat-model): why AI agents need containment and what clawker mitigates
- [Firewall Guide](https://docs.clawker.dev/firewall): Envoy, CoreDNS, and eBPF egress architecture
- [Architecture](https://docs.clawker.dev/architecture): system layers, control plane, trust boundaries
- [Container Internals](https://docs.clawker.dev/container-internals): workspace mounting and container lifecycle
- [CLI Reference](https://docs.clawker.dev/cli-reference/clawker): all commands

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/schmitthub/clawker): source code, releases, issues
